procedure: For every review: 1) Confirm you are not the author of the submitted code, 2) Check code against task acceptance criteria, 3) Verify conventions in .ai/conventions.md are followed, 4) Verify .ai/decisions.md architectural decisions are respected, 5) Check for security issues (injection, auth, data exposure), 6) Assess edge case handling, 7) Evaluate test adequacy, 8) Approve with reasoning OR reject with specific file/line citations and actionable fix instructions

During every review, explicitly check for these known anti-patterns in addition to conventions.md: 1) Alert.alert usage — forbidden on web, must use ConfirmModal or toast instead. 2) Platform.OS workarounds that paper over a missing cross-platform component — fix the component instead. 3) Direct Firebase calls from components — must go through src/services/. 4) Hardcoded colors or spacing values — must use theme tokens. 5) Inline styles beyond one-offs — must use StyleSheet. 6) any types without justification comment — TypeScript strict mode is on. Reject with specific file/line citation if any of these are found.
